transcript: Outcome, positive sales rep, hi Sarah, this is Mike from TaskFlow. I'm following up on your request for information about our project management tool. How are things going today? Customer, hi Mike, doing well thanks. Yes, we are currently looking for a better way to track our engineering sprints. Right now, we're using Tira, but it's getting too complicated for our non-technical team members. Sales rep, that's a very common frustration. Tira is powerful, but it definitely has a steep learning curve. TaskFlow is built specifically to bridge that gap, giving engineers the structure they need while keeping the interface clean for marketing and product teams. Customer, that sounds good on paper, but Tira has a lot of advanced reporting features we rely on for our weekly executive updates. Does TaskFlow have similar reporting depth? Sales rep, we do. We have built an executive dashboards that you can customize with drag and drop widgets and you can export them directly to PDF or share a live link. It actually takes fewer clicks than setting it up in Tira. Customer, okay, that sounds promising. How easy is it to migrate our current active projects over? We can't afford to lose our history. Sales rep, we have a native tier importer that brings over all tasks, comments, and attachments in about 10 minutes. I can show you exactly how it works on a quick video call. How does this Thursday at 2 p.m. sound? Customer, Thursday works for me. Let's do it. Send over the calendar invite. Sales rep, perfect, I'll send that right through. Looking forward to showing you around, Sarah. Have a great day. Customer, thanks, Mike. Talk Thursday.

Outcome, negative sales rep, Hi Patrick, this is Chris from Desk Support. I'm checking in on your evaluation of our helpdesk software. I know you were comparing us with a few other options. Customer, oh, hi Chris. Thanks for reaching out. We actually made a decision yesterday and ended up signing a contract with HelpScout instead. Sales rep, I see. I appreciate you letting me know. If you don't mind me asking, what was the deciding factor that pushed you toward HelpScout? Customer, it mostly came down to their native integration with our CRM. Your team required a third-party connector tool, which meant an extra cost and more room for errors. Sales rep, ah, understood. Our native CRM integration is actually launching next quarter, but I know you needed a solution today. Customer, yeah, we couldn't wait around for the roadmap, unfortunately. We needed something working immediately. Sales rep, that makes complete sense. HelpScout is a great tool and a seamless CRM connection is critical. I wish you and your team the best of luck with the rollout. Customer, thanks, Chris. I appreciate your time over the last few weeks. Sales rep, anytime, Patrick. Take care. Customer, you too, bye.

Outcome, positive sales rep, hi Janet, this is Mark from Signify HR. I'm following up on the platform overview I emailed you last Tuesday. Have you had a chance to look it over? Customer, hi Mark. Yes, I did. The performance tracking modules look exactly like what our managers have been asking for. It's very intuitive. Sales rep, that's fantastic to hear. We really focused on making the review process less painful for busy managers. Customer, my main hesitation right now is the setup. Our IT team is completely buried with an infrastructure migration, and they told me they won't have time to help us plug this in. Sales rep, I completely get it, IT bottlenecks are tough. The good news is Signify HR is entirely cloud-based and requires zero technical installation or code. We handle all the initial employee data uploads for you. Customer, wait, so my IT team doesn't need to write any APIs or configure servers? Sales rep, not at all. As long as you can export a basic CSV employee list, our team handles the rest. You can be up and running by next Monday without opening a single IT ticket. Customer, wow, that's a massive relief. If your team does the heavy lifting on the data, I'm ready to start. Sales rep, love to hear it. Let's get the trial account upgraded to a pilot. I'll send over the launch paperwork right now. Customer, perfect, send it over and I'll get it signed today.

Outcome, Neutral Sales Rep, Hello Trevor, this is Liam from OptiRoute. I saw your logistics team recently expanded your delivery fleet and wanted to see how you're optimizing your daily routes. Customer, Hi Liam. We actually just onboarded RouteMaster about six months ago. It's handling our multi-stop routing pretty well so far. Sales Rep, RouteMaster is a decent tool for standard mapping. Where our clients see a massive difference with OptiRoute is our live traffic rerouting and predictive ETAs, which usually cuts fuel costs by another 15%. Customer, a 15% drop in fuel would be huge, honestly. Our current tool doesn't account for real-time traffic changes very well. Sales Rep, that's exactly what we specialize in. I'd love to take three of your typical delivery addresses and run a quick, live optimization comparison for you on a short call. Customer, I'm definitely curious to see that, but I'm locked into an annual contract with RouteMaster for another six months. I don't want to waste your time if I can't buy today. Sales Rep, you wouldn't be wasting my time at all. If the numbers make sense, you'll have the data ready for your Q4 budgeting cycle. How does this Wednesday at 11am look? Customer, fair point. Let's do Wednesday at 11. It's worth seeing if the savings justified breaking a contract early. Sales Rep, excellent. I will send a calendar invite with a link. Speak on Wednesday, Trevor. Customer, sounds good, Liam. Bye.

Outcome, negative sales rep, hi Monica, this is Chloe from SecureVault. I wanted to follow up on your security team's evaluation of our cloud backup platform. Customer, hi Chloe, thanks for calling. We actually finished our review of cloud vendors yesterday afternoon. Sales rep, great. How did SecureVault stack up against the other options you were looking at? Customer, unfortunately, we decided to go with Cybershield instead. Their platform just fits our current technical stack a bit better. Sales rep, I appreciate the transparency. If you don't mind sharing, was there a specific feature or integration that tilted the scales toward them? Customer, it came down to their native integration with AWS. Your platform required us to set up a custom gateway, and Cybershield just works out of the box with our existing setup. Sales rep, ah, I understand. Minimizing setup friction is incredibly important. We are launching an AWS native connector later this year, but I know you needed it ready today. Customer, exactly. We couldn't delay the rollout waiting on a roadmap item. Sales rep, totally fair. Cybershield is a strong platform, and you made a solid choice for your architecture. I appreciate your time throughout the trial, Monica. Customer, thanks, Chloe. I appreciate your help as well. Take care.

Outcome, positive sales rep, hi Derek, this is Nina from Papels. I'm following up on the custom pricing quote I sent over for our merchant processing software last week. Customer, hi Nina. I went over the numbers with our operations director. The platform features look great, but the transaction fees are a bit higher than we anticipated. Sales rep, I appreciate you being upfront about that. Which specific tier or fee structure felt like a stretch for your margins? Customer, it's the 2.9% fee on our high volume international transactions. We do a lot of cross-border sales, and that fee is going to eat into our profits significantly. Sales rep, that is a completely fair point, Derek. Because your international volume is so high, we can actually move you to our preferred merchant tier, which drops that specific rate down to 2.2%. Customer, really? What do we have to do to qualify for that lower rate? Sales rep, we just need to commit to a 12-month agreement instead of the month-to-month plan. Based on your current volume, that change alone will save you roughly $800 a month. Customer, oh wow. A 12-month commitment is fine by us since we don't plan on switching anyway. That rate change makes a huge difference. Sales rep, fantastic. I'll update the agreement with the 2.2% international rate and send it over for your signature. Customer, perfect. Send it through and I'll get our CFO to sign off on it right now.

Outcome, Neutral Sales Rep. Hello Priya, this is Sam from TalentScout. I wanted to check in on the recruitment automation software proposal we discussed last Thursday. Customer, Hi Sam. I actually just got out of a meeting with our HR director. We really love the automated screening features, but we've run into a roadblock. Sales Rep, Oh, I'm sorry to hear that. What kind of roadblock are you facing? Customer, our department's software budget for this half of the year was completely maxed out by our new payroll system upgrade. We literally don't have the funds left for any new tools right now. Sales Rep, budget caps are always a tough hurdle. Since our fiscal year wraps up next month, what if we deferred your first invoice until the start of the next quarter, but got you on boarded now? Customer, that's a creative solution, but our internal policy won't let us sign a contract without the active budget allocation already approved in the CRM. Sales Rep understood compliance rules are there for a reason. If we paused until your new budget opens up in July, would Talent Scout still be your team's top choice? Customer, absolutely. It's exactly what we need. It's just a matter of timing at this point. Sales Rep, perfect. Let's do this. I'll hold our current promotional pricing for you, and I'll send a brief email in late June to see if the new budget is unlocked. How does that sound? Customer, that sounds perfect, Sam. Let's reconnect in June. Sales Rep, will do, Priya. Have a wonderful week.

Outcome, positive sales rep, Hi Arthur, this is Grace from Lobytrack. I saw you were testing out our warehouse scanning application yesterday. How did the scanning speeds look? Customer, Hi Grace. The speeds are lightning fast and the barcode recognition is excellent. However, I noticed it doesn't seem to support Matrix barcode scanning for our medical inventory. Sales rep, your spot on, Arthur. By default, the trial version only has standard 1D and 2D QR codes enabled to keep the interface simple. Matrix scanning is an optional module we toggle on. Customer, oh. So your system can actually scan data matrix codes without us needing a third-party app? Sales rep, yes, absolutely. It's a simple toggle on our back end. It formats the matrix data directly into your inventory fields just like standard barcodes. Customer, okay, that changes things completely. We almost crossed you off the list because we thought that feature was missing. Sales rep, I'm so glad we cleared that up. I can activate that matrix module on your live trial account right now so your warehouse team can test it out this afternoon. Customer, please do. If that matrix scanning works smoothly on our medical boxes today, we're ready to buy. Sales rep, done, it is now active on your account. I'll follow up with you tomorrow morning to see how the test went and drop the subscription link. Customer, excellent. Thanks, Grace. Talk tomorrow.

Outcome, negative sales rep. Hello Victor, this is Diana from Site Monitor. I'm calling to see if you've had a chance to look over the website uptime and analytics contract we sent over? Customer, hi Diana. Yes, the contract arrived. But honestly, I haven't even had five minutes to open the PDF. Things have been completely chaotic on our engineering side. Sales rep, I totally understand, uptime emergencies wait for no one. Is there a quick summary or a specific clause I can run through right now to save you some time? Customer, no, it's not the contract terms themselves. Our main server cluster suffered a massive outage over the weekend, and my entire team is working 14 hour days just to rebuild our database infrastructure. Sales rep, wow, that sounds incredibly stressful. I completely get why a new vendor contract is the last thing on your mind right now. Customer, yeah, realistically, I cannot look at this or talk to any new vendors for at least the next month or two. Our focus is 100% on internal recovery. Sales rep, that makes total sense. Your current infrastructure has to take priority. I'll take a big step back and let your team do what they need to do. Customer, I appreciate that, Diana. Thanks for understanding. Sales rep, of course, Victor. Wishing you a smooth recovery with the servers. I'll check back in late summer once the dust has settled. Customer, sounds good. Goodbye.

Outcome, Neutral Sales Rep, Hi Brenda, this is Ethan from DeskFlow. I'm following up on our successful demo last week. Are your customer service leads still excited about the ticketing automation? Customer, Hi Ethan. Yes, the team is still raving about how much time it will save them on canned responses. It's definitely a hit with the frontline staff. Sales Rep, that's amazing feedback. Since the team is on board, are we good to move forward with setting up your instance? Customer, I wish I could say yes, but I ran the pricing proposal past our regional director, and he wants a full breakdown of how this software will impact our resolution times before he signs off. Sales Rep, that's a very reasonable request from leadership. We actually have a standardized impact report based on case studies from similar customer service teams. Would that help you present the case? Customer, yes, that would be incredibly helpful. If I can show him a clear estimate of the time to resolution drop, it'll make getting his approval much easier. Sales Rep, I will email that impact report over to you immediately. Would it make sense for us to schedule a brief 5 minute call with your director next week to address any other concerns he has? Customer, let me give him the report first and see what he says. I'll email you on Friday to let you know if we need a joint call. Sales Rep, sounds like a plan, Brenda. The report is on its way to your inbox now. Customer, great, thanks Ethan. Speak soon.

Outcome, Negative Sales Rep, Hi Craig, this is Julia from Content Hub. I noticed you downloaded our digital asset management white paper and wanted to see if your creative team was looking for a better way to organize files. Customer, Hi Julia. Yes, we were looking around, but honestly, our current setup with Google Drive is working perfectly fine for us right now. Sales Rep, Google Drive is great for basic storage, but many creative teams switch to Content Hub because we offer automated asset tagging and direct integration with Adobe apps, which saves hours of searching. Customer, I understand, but our team is tiny, there are only three of us. We have a highly organized folder structure, so we never really lose track of our files anyway. Sales Rep, that makes sense for a smaller team. If you plan on scaling your content production next year, having an enterprise grade asset manager early on can prevent a lot of headaches down the road. Customer, we don't have any expansion plans for the foreseeable future. Google Drive costs us almost nothing, and a migration sounds like an unnecessary chore for a problem we don't have. Sales Rep, I completely respect that, Craig. If things are running smoothly, there's no need to disrupt your workflow. I appreciate you taking the time to chat. Customer, thanks for being understanding, Julia. Have a good day. Sales Rep, you too, Craig. Take care.

Outcome, negative sales rep. Hello David, this is Amanda from SecureSign. I noticed your team has been expanding its HR department and I wanted to see how you are currently handling offer letters and contract signing. Customer, Hi Amanda. We actually use SignEasy right now and it's fully integrated into our HR system. It's working perfectly fine for us. Sales rep, SignEasy is a solid tool. Many of our current clients used them previously but switched to SecureSign because we offer unlimited templates and automated SMS reminders which speeds up completion rates by about 40%. Customer, honestly, our completion rate is already over 90% and our contract with them doesn't expire for another 9 months. We don't really have any incentive to move. Sales rep, I completely understand. If you could get the same functionality plus automated compliance tracking for 20% less than your current rate would that be worth a look for your next cycle? Customer, not right now. Any change requires IT approval and retraining for 50 recruiters which is a massive headache. We're happy with what we have. Sales rep, fair enough. I appreciate your honesty, David. Would it be okay if I checked back in with you closer to your renewal date next year? Customer, sure, you can send me an email next spring but I can't promise anything will change. Sales rep, no problem at all. I'll send over a quick resource via email for your records. Have a good week. Customer, thanks, Amanda. Bye.

Outcome, positive sales rep, Hi Natalie, this is Ryan from SurveyPulse. I saw you signed up for a trial account this morning and wanted to welcome you to the platform. Customer, oh, hi Ryan. Thanks for reaching out. I was actually just playing around with the survey builder. The logic jumping features are incredibly smooth. Sales rep, I'm glad you like the interface. We tried to make complex survey logic as visual and simple as possible. What kind of project are you launching? Customer, we are doing a massive quarterly feedback push for our user base next month. My only concern is the participant pricing tier. The jump from 5,000 to 10,000 responses is a bit steep for our budget. Sales rep, ah, I see what you mean. If you're right on the edge of that tier, we can actually create a custom middle plan for 7,500 responses that bridges that price gap perfectly for your project. Customer, oh, I didn't know you did custom response caps. That would actually fit our budget perfectly. Sales rep, we absolutely do. If we lock in that 7,500 response tier today, I can have our success team help you format your user list so it's ready to launch smoothly. Customer, wow, that's great service. Yes, let's do that. Can you send over the custom plan upgrade link? Sales rep, I'll generate that custom billing link in email at over right now. Welcome to Survey Pulse, Natalie. Customer, thanks, Ryan. Excited to get this survey launched.

Outcome, Neutral Sales Rep, Hi Elena, John here from Cloud Inventory. I saw that you downloaded our retail inventory guide last week. Are you experiencing some challenges with stock tracking at your warehouses? Customer, Hi John. Yes, we are opening two new locations, and our current spreadsheets just aren't cutting it anymore. We keep running into stock outs. Sales Rep, spreadsheets get overwhelming very fast when you scale. Cloud Inventory gives you real-time counts across all locations and sinks automatically with your point of sale system. Customer, I looked at your pricing page earlier, though. Your mid-tier plan is $450 a month per location. That seems extremely high for an inventory app. Sales Rep, I hear you, the upfront cost can look significant. But when you factor in the cost of lost sales from stock outs and the hours spent manually updating spreadsheets, most stores save over $1,200 a month. Customer, that might be true, but as a growing retail business, our cash flow is tight right now. $900 a month for two new locations is a tough pill to swallow. Sales Rep, what if we looked at a quarterly billing cycle instead of annual, or started you on our core plan at $250 a month to get your feet wet? Customer, the core plan might work, but I need to sit down and map out our exact budget for next month first. Sales Rep, that makes total sense. How about I check back in with you next Tuesday to see where you stand on those numbers? Customer, yes, let's do next Tuesday. Send me an email morning of to confirm. Sales Rep, we'll do Alayna. Speak next week. Customer, sounds good, thanks John.

Outcome, Positive Sales Rep, Hi Robert, this is Rachel from HR Cloud. I'm following up on the trial account you created last week. How are you finding the platform so far? Customer, Hi Rachel. The interface looks beautiful, and it seems to have everything we need for shift scheduling and time tracking. Sales Rep, that's great to hear. Was there anything specific you were hoping to test out during the trial that you haven't gotten to yet? Customer, my main worry is the actual rollout. We have about 80 employees, and half of them are out in the field and aren't very tech savvy. I'm terrified they won't adopt it and will waste our money. Sales Rep, that is a completely valid fear. To make sure that doesn't happen, we assign a dedicated implementation manager to your account. They run live training webinars for your staff and provide short video guides they can watch on their phones. Customer, okay, that alleviates some stress. Is there an extra fee for that onboarding support? Sales Rep, it's completely included in our annual team plan. We handle the heavy lifting of importing your employee data too, so you don't have to manually type anything. Customer, wow, that takes a lot off my plate. If you can handle the data migration, I'm a lot more comfortable moving forward. Sales Rep, absolutely, we do it all the time. Why don't we schedule a 15-minute call with our onboarding specialist to map out a timeline for your team? Customer, yes, let's do that. Can we do Wednesday morning? Sales Rep, Wednesday at 10 am works perfectly. I'll send over the meeting link right now. Customer, great, see you then.

Outcome, negative sales rep, hi Marcus, this is Kevin from Lead Genius. We spoke a couple of weeks ago about your goals for increasing outbound pipeline for your sales team. Customer, oh, hey Kevin. Yes, I remember. Thanks for calling. Sales rep, great. I put together a custom package based on your target market that would give your reps 500 verified leads per month. I wanted to see if you were ready to kick this off. Customer, to be honest with you, Kevin, our corporate office just announced a company wide freeze on all new software spending for the rest of the quarter. Sales rep, wow, I'm sorry to hear that. Is that across all departments, or is there any flexibility for tools that directly drive revenue? Customer, it's a hard freeze across the board. Even if I wanted this tool, and my team definitely needs it, I literally cannot get a corporate credit card approved right now. Sales rep, understood. Budgets can be unpredictable. Since the quarter ends in six weeks, does it make sense for us to reconnect early next quarter? Customer, honestly, looking at the forecasts, I think this freeze will likely extend into the next half of the year. It's better if we don't waste your time right now. Sales rep, I appreciate you being upfront about that, Marcus. I'll take a step back and let things settle on your end. I'll keep an eye on your company's updates. Customer, thanks for understanding, Kevin. Best of luck.

Outcome, positive sales rep, hi Jessica, this is Tom from Chatbot Pro. I saw your team is handling a huge volume of customer support tickets on your website and wanted to show you how our AI bot can automate half of those. Customer, hi Tom. We actually looked at your tool last month, but we had to pass because your platform didn't support WhatsApp integration. Most of our international customers reach out through there. Sales rep, I have some great news on that front. We actually launched our native WhatsApp business integration just last week. It allows the bot to handle WhatsApp chat seamlessly alongside your website widget. Customer, oh really? That's interesting. Can the bot route the conversation to a live agent via WhatsApp if it gets stuck? Sales rep, yes, exactly. If the AI can't answer a query, it flags a human agent who can jump right into the chat thread from our unified inbox. Customer, okay, that changes things. That was our single biggest blocker. Sales rep, I'd love to show you a quick demo of the live WhatsApp flow. Do you have 15 minutes tomorrow afternoon? Customer, let me check my calendar. Yeah, I can do 3.30pm tomorrow. Sales rep, perfect. I will send over a Zoom link right away. Looking forward to chatting tomorrow. Customer, sounds good, see you tomorrow.

Outcome, Neutral Sales Rep, Hi Brian, this is Sophia from FinTrack. I sent over the contract proposal for our expense management platform on Friday and wanted to see if you had a chance to review it. Customer, Hi Sophia. Yes, I received it. I skimmed through the terms, but honestly, I haven't had a single free hour to sit down and read it carefully with my legal team. It's been chaotic here. Sales Rep, I completely understand, end of month is always crazy for finance teams. Is there any particular section you or your legal team have questions about that I can clarify right now? Customer, no, it's nothing specific. We just have an internal policy that all multi-year software agreements must be thoroughly vetted by legal, and they have a two-week backlog right now. Sales Rep, that makes sense. We want to make sure your legal team is 100% comfortable. To keep things moving, would it help if I sent over our standard data privacy writer that usually answers most legal questions up front? Customer, yeah, that actually might speed things up for them. Send that over. Sales Rep, done. I will send that immediately. Should I follow up with you in about two weeks once they've had a chance to breathe? Customer, yes, give it two weeks. If they finish early, I'll let you know. Sales Rep, perfect. Thanks for your time, Brian. Talk soon. Customer, thanks, Sophia. Bye.

Outcome, Neutral Sales Rep. Hello David, this is Lisa from EDU Learn. We did a demo of our employee training platform last week, and I wanted to see what your thoughts were after sleeping on it. Customer, Hi Lisa. I still think the platform is fantastic. The micro learning modules are exactly what our team needs to complete their annual compliance training. Sales Rep, Awesome. I know you mentioned your VP of operations needs to give the final sign off. Have you had a chance to present the business case to them yet? Customer, I sent in the pitch deck you gave me, but he replied saying he wants to understand the ROI better before he signs the purchase order. He's very data driven. Sales Rep, That's standard for VPs. I actually have a one page ROI calculator spreadsheet that uses your team's size to estimate the hours and compliance fines saved. Would that help? Customer, Yes, Absolutely. If I can show him hard numbers, it's going to make my life a lot easier. Sales Rep, I'll email that to you right now. Would it be beneficial if I joined a brief 10 minute call with you and your VP to walk him through the numbers directly? Customer, let me show him the spreadsheet first. If he has tough questions, I'll pull you in for a quick call next week. Sales Rep, Sounds like a solid plan. I'll send the email now and follow up with you on Monday. Customer, Perfect. Thanks Lisa.

Outcome, positive sales rep, hi Karen, this is James from ReviewPulse. I noticed you've been collecting a lot of great customer feedback manually, and I wanted to see if you're ready to automate your review collection with us? Customer, hi James. We love the tool, but your annual plan requires upfront payment, and our current cash flow doesn't allow us to drop $2,400 all at once right now. Sales rep, I completely understand. Upfront annual costs can be a barrier for growing brands. We do offer a monthly payment option, though it doesn't include the 15% annual discount. Customer, how much would the monthly option come out to? Sales rep, it breaks down to $230 per month, build monthly, and you can cancel anytime you need to. Customer, $230 a month is definitely a number I can fit into our operating budget without needing special approval. Sales rep, perfect. If we set you up on the monthly plan today, you can start automatically collecting reviews from this week's customers by tonight. Customer, let's do it. Can you send over the link to input our credit card details for the monthly plan? Sales rep, sending it over via email right now. Let me know if you hit any snags while setting it up. Customer, got it, opening the link now. Thanks, James. Sales rep, you're welcome, Karen. Welcome to ReviewPulse.

अख़ उगड़ तरदी बच्छटवार लेंप थुज्ट्टिएश. समवाद डयालोग लेंठ थुज्टिएश. सिल्स पर्सं अमित व्यापार प्लस् ग्राहक राजेश शर्मा किराना स्टोर. बाज्चीत आमित नमस्कार क्या मेरी बाज्च्टषर्मा किराना स्टोर के मालिक राजेश जी से हो रही है? राजेश खाए मैं राजेश बोर रहा हूं. बताये क्या काम है? आमित राजेश जी मैं व्यापार प्लस् से बाथ कर रहा हूं. हमारी कमपनी कुदरा दुकानो के लिये धिजिटल भिलिंग और इनवेंटरी सुफ्ट्ट्वार बनाती है, जिस से आपका हिसाप किताब आसान हो जाएगा. राजेश भाई साहब, हमारे पास इतना समय नहीं है. और सच बताओ तो अभी हमें इसकी कोई जरूरत भी महसुस नहीं हो रही है. अमित मैं आपकी बाथ समच्छ सकता हूं राजेश जी. लेकिन इस सुफ्ट्वेर से आपका रोस का देड गन्ता बचेगा जो स्टोक मिलाने मी जाता है. आप बस पाच मिनद्का समय दें. राजेश देखिये बाथ समय की नहीं है. अभी दुकान का बजट बहुत तन चल रहा है. इस महीने हम कोई नया करच नहीं उठासकते. अमित सर यह करच नहीं बलकी निवेष है. हम आप को पहले पंडरा दिन का मुझ्त्र तरायल दे रहे है. अगर पसंद ना आए, तो आप पैसे मत दीजीएगा. राजेश अच्छा. पंडरा दिन के लिए सच में कोई पैसा नहीं कटेगा. कोई चिपा हुए चारच तो नहीं है न. अमित बilkul नहीं सर कोई हिडन चारच नहीं है. कल सुबहा हमारे एक जी कुटिव आप की दुकान पर आखर सबसेट कर देंगे. क्या सुबहा जारा बजे का समझ थीक रहेगा? राजेश थीक है, कल सुबहा जारा बजे बहेज दीजीए. देख लेते है कि यह हमारे काम आता है यह नहीं. दन्यवाद अबजेक्षन बजेट इश्व, बजजट की समस्या संटिमेंट अज़ितिव सकारात्मक रिस्क लेवल ख्लो ख्लो कम
